CREATE TABLE test_dates (date_time DateTime) ENGINE = Memory;
SELECT addMilliseconds(date_time, 1000) AS add_milliseconds FROM test_dates;
WITH toDateTime('2024-01-01 00:00:00') AS date_time SELECT addMilliseconds(date_time, 500) AS add_milliseconds_500;
SELECT addMilliseconds(date_time, -1000) AS subtract_milliseconds FROM test_dates;
WITH '2024-01-01 00:00:00' AS date_time_string SELECT addMilliseconds(date_time_string, 2000) AS add_milliseconds_string;
WITH (SELECT toDateTime('2024-01-01 00:00:00')) AS subquery SELECT addMilliseconds(subquery, 3000) AS add_milliseconds_subquery;
CREATE TABLE timestamps (event_time DateTime) ENGINE = Memory;
SELECT addMilliseconds(event_time, 60000) AS event_plus_60s FROM timestamps;
SELECT t.event_time, addMilliseconds(t.event_time, 120000) AS event_plus_2m FROM timestamps AS t WHERE event_time < toDateTime('2024-01-01 02:00:00');
WITH (SELECT toDateTime('2024-01-01 00:00:00') AS time, 1000 AS ms) AS params SELECT addMilliseconds(params.time, params.ms) AS updated_time;
CREATE TABLE combined_dates (start_time DateTime, end_time DateTime) ENGINE = Memory;
SELECT start_time, end_time, addMilliseconds(start_time, 120000) AS start_plus_2m FROM combined_dates WHERE addMilliseconds(end_time, -60000) > start_time;
